content.text: I'll quickly investigate the README and test setup, then file the issue.

content.content:
1	# netshooter
2	
3	[![AWS server](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsedemon/netshooter/badges/status.json)](https://github.com/pulsedemon/netshooter/actions/workflows/deploy-aws.yml)
4	
5	Fast 3D shooter/melee game in development. This milestone is a **networked movement
6	vertical slice**: players on different internet connections see each other move
7	in real time, server-authoritative, over Tailscale.
8	
9	## Run it
10	
11	1. Install [Tailscale](https://tailscale.com/download) on every player's machine
12	   and sign in (the free plan is enough). Confirm with `tailscale status`.
13	2. On the server machine, start the dedicated server:
14	   `./start_server.sh`
15	3. Share the server machine's Tailscale IP (the `100.x.y.z` one) with players.
16	4. On each client machine, open the project in Godot 4.7 and press Play (F5), or run:
17	   `just run` (or raw: `/Applications/Godot.app/Contents/MacOS/Godot --path .`)
18	5. Each player types the server IP into the field and clicks **Join**.
19	6. Everyone should see each other's capsules. WASD to move, mouse to look,
20	   space to jump.
21	
22	Up to 4 players.
23	
24	### Players on different Tailscale accounts
25	
26	A Tailscale tailnet is single-account: machines on **different** accounts can't
27	see each other by default, and a join to an unreachable server will hang on
28	"Connecting...". If your players don't share one account, share the server machine
29	across accounts:
30	
31	1. Server runner: in the [Tailscale admin console](https://login.tailscale.com/admin/machines),
32	   open the server machine → **Share** → copy the invite link.
33	2. Send the link to the other player.
34	3. The other player opens it while signed into **their own** Tailscale account
35	   and accepts the share.
36	4. Verify on each machine that `tailscale status` now lists the other, and that
37	   `tailscale ping <other-100.x-ip>` succeeds.
38	5. The peer can now **Join** using the server's `100.x` IP.
39	
40	If `tailscale status` shows only your own machine, the peer isn't reachable yet —
41	fix that before trying to connect in-game.
42	
43	## Troubleshooting
44	
45	**Frame stutter when firing / moving the mouse (macOS):** the window-snapping
46	app [Magnet](https://magnet.crowdcafe.com/) has been observed causing a per-frame
47	hitch that looks like the game lagging. Its drag-to-edge snap detection reacts to
48	the cursor reaching a screen edge — which happens constantly during fast
49	mouse-look. The symptom is distinctive: it only affects mouse players (not
50	controllers) and persists every frame, regardless of how powerful the machine is.
51	If you see this, quit Magnet while playing (or exclude the game / disable its snap
52	zones). Other window-snappers vary — Rectangle, for instance, has *not* reproduced
53	this — but if you run one and see the symptom, try disabling it as a first step.
54	
55	## Developer setup
56	
57	Install [`just`](https://github.com/casey/just) (`brew install just` on macOS) —
58	it's the command front door for this repo. Run `just` to list all commands.
59	Common ones: `just run` (launch the game), `just test` (run the suite),
60	`just check` (the full pre-PR preflight CI also runs), `just hooks` (install the
61	pre-commit hooks below). `just run`/`just edit` are GUI launches that block the
62	terminal until the window closes — that's expected, not a hang.
63	
64	The Godot binary is located by `bin/godot`; set `GODOT_BIN` to override
65	auto-detection if your install isn't on the default path.
66	
67	After cloning, install the pre-commit hooks once. They run `gdlint` and `gdformat`
68	on staged GDScript before each commit, so formatting/lint issues are caught locally
69	instead of on CI:
70	
71	```bash
72	pip install pre-commit   # if you don't already have it
73	pre-commit install
74	```
75	
76	Recommended but not mandatory: **CI runs the same `gdlint`/`gdformat` checks on
77	every pull request**, so skipping the hook means a red check on your PR rather than
78	broken `master`. GDScript is linted and formatted with
79	[gdtoolkit](https://github.com/Scony/godot-gdscript-toolkit); rules live in
80	`.gdlintrc`, pinned to one version across `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and
81	`.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
82	
83	The `addons/` directory is gitignored except `addons/gd-plug/` (the gd-plug
84	package manager itself). gdUnit4 is installed on demand from the pinned root
85	`plug.gd` — `just test` and `just edit` run `just install` automatically, so a
86	fresh clone fetches it on first use (or run `just install` directly).
87	
88	## Testing
89	
90	Unit tests (GdUnit4):
91	
92	```bash
93	just test                 # full suite
94	just test test_weapon     # one suite
95	
96	# Raw equivalent (no just):
97	/Applications/Godot.app/Contents/MacOS/Godot --headless --path . \
98	  -s addons/gdUnit4/bin/GdUnitCmdTool.gd --ignoreHeadlessMode -a tests
99	```
100	
101	Local test: start the dedicated server, then launch one or more clients and join
102	`127.0.0.1`.
103	
104	## Architecture
105	
106	Server-authoritative over `ENetMultiplayerPeer` (port 7777). Clients send input
107	via RPC; the dedicated server simulates and replicates transforms with
108	`MultiplayerSynchronizer`; the owned client predicts and reconciles its own
109	movement. Tailscale provides NAT punch-through so internet peers connect as if
110	on a LAN. See `docs/superpowers/specs/` and `docs/superpowers/plans/`.
111	
112	For the AWS dedicated server deployment path, see `infra/README.md`.
113

file.content:
# netshooter

[![AWS server](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pulsedemon/netshooter/badges/status.json)](https://github.com/pulsedemon/netshooter/actions/workflows/deploy-aws.yml)

Fast 3D shooter/melee game in development. This milestone is a **networked movement
vertical slice**: players on different internet connections see each other move
in real time, server-authoritative, over Tailscale.

## Run it

1. Install [Tailscale](https://tailscale.com/download) on every player's machine
   and sign in (the free plan is enough). Confirm with `tailscale status`.
2. On the server machine, start the dedicated server:
   `./start_server.sh`
3. Share the server machine's Tailscale IP (the `100.x.y.z` one) with players.
4. On each client machine, open the project in Godot 4.7 and press Play (F5), or run:
   `just run` (or raw: `/Applications/Godot.app/Contents/MacOS/Godot --path .`)
5. Each player types the server IP into the field and clicks **Join**.
6. Everyone should see each other's capsules. WASD to move, mouse to look,
   space to jump.

Up to 4 players.

### Players on different Tailscale accounts

A Tailscale tailnet is single-account: machines on **different** accounts can't
see each other by default, and a join to an unreachable server will hang on
"Connecting...". If your players don't share one account, share the server machine
across accounts:

1. Server runner: in the [Tailscale admin console](https://login.tailscale.com/admin/machines),
   open the server machine → **Share** → copy the invite link.
2. Send the link to the other player.
3. The other player opens it while signed into **their own** Tailscale account
   and accepts the share.
4. Verify on each machine that `tailscale status` now lists the other, and that
   `tailscale ping <other-100.x-ip>` succeeds.
5. The peer can now **Join** using the server's `100.x` IP.

If `tailscale status` shows only your own machine, the peer isn't reachable yet —
fix that before trying to connect in-game.

## Troubleshooting

**Frame stutter when firing / moving the mouse (macOS):** the window-snapping
app [Magnet](https://magnet.crowdcafe.com/) has been observed causing a per-frame
hitch that looks like the game lagging. Its drag-to-edge snap detection reacts to
the cursor reaching a screen edge — which happens constantly during fast
mouse-look. The symptom is distinctive: it only affects mouse players (not
controllers) and persists every frame, regardless of how powerful the machine is.
If you see this, quit Magnet while playing (or exclude the game / disable its snap
zones). Other window-snappers vary — Rectangle, for instance, has *not* reproduced
this — but if you run one and see the symptom, try disabling it as a first step.

## Developer setup

Install [`just`](https://github.com/casey/just) (`brew install just` on macOS) —
it's the command front door for this repo. Run `just` to list all commands.
Common ones: `just run` (launch the game), `just test` (run the suite),
`just check` (the full pre-PR preflight CI also runs), `just hooks` (install the
pre-commit hooks below). `just run`/`just edit` are GUI launches that block the
terminal until the window closes — that's expected, not a hang.

The Godot binary is located by `bin/godot`; set `GODOT_BIN` to override
auto-detection if your install isn't on the default path.

After cloning, install the pre-commit hooks once. They run `gdlint` and `gdformat`
on staged GDScript before each commit, so formatting/lint issues are caught locally
instead of on CI:

```bash
pip install pre-commit   # if you don't already have it
pre-commit install
```

Recommended but not mandatory: **CI runs the same `gdlint`/`gdformat` checks on
every pull request**, so skipping the hook means a red check on your PR rather than
broken `master`. GDScript is linted and formatted with
[gdtoolkit](https://github.com/Scony/godot-gdscript-toolkit); rules live in
`.gdlintrc`, pinned to one version across `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and
`.github/workflows/ci.yml`.

The `addons/` directory is gitignored except `addons/gd-plug/` (the gd-plug
package manager itself). gdUnit4 is installed on demand from the pinned root
`plug.gd` — `just test` and `just edit` run `just install` automatically, so a
fresh clone fetches it on first use (or run `just install` directly).

## Testing

Unit tests (GdUnit4):

```bash
just test                 # full suite
just test test_weapon     # one suite

# Raw equivalent (no just):
/Applications/Godot.app/Contents/MacOS/Godot --headless --path . \
  -s addons/gdUnit4/bin/GdUnitCmdTool.gd --ignoreHeadlessMode -a tests
```

Local test: start the dedicated server, then launch one or more clients and join
`127.0.0.1`.

## Architecture

Server-authoritative over `ENetMultiplayerPeer` (port 7777). Clients send input
via RPC; the dedicated server simulates and replicates transforms with
`MultiplayerSynchronizer`; the owned client predicts and reconciles its own
movement. Tailscale provides NAT punch-through so internet peers connect as if
on a LAN. See `docs/superpowers/specs/` and `docs/superpowers/plans/`.

For the AWS dedicated server deployment path, see `infra/README.md`.

content.content:
# netshooter dev commands. Run `just` to list. Requires `just` (brew install just)
# and Godot resolvable by bin/godot (set GODOT_BIN to override).
set shell := ["bash", "-euc"]

# Tracked GDScript, excluding addons/ — matches CI's file selection. This skips
# third-party addon code (gdUnit4) and our gd-plug fork, which we keep close to
# its upstream shape rather than reformatting to our lint rules.
gd_files := "git ls-files '*.gd' ':!:addons/**'"

# Default: list all recipes.
default:
	@just --list

# Launch the game directly (no editor). Blocks until the window closes.
run:
	bin/godot --path .

# Open the project in the Godot editor. Blocks until the editor closes.
edit: install
	bin/godot -e --path .

# Run the dedicated headless server. Forwards args, e.g. `just server --smoke-test`.
server *ARGS:
	./start_server.sh {{ARGS}}

# Run the GdUnit4 suite headless. `just test` runs all; `just test test_weapon`
# runs one suite (with or without the .gd / tests/ prefix). `install` runs first
# (recipe dependency). Cold-path defenses (fatal import, zero-suite sentinel, runner
# exit-code propagation) live in bin/run-tests.sh, which CI runs too.
test suite="tests": install
	bin/run-tests.sh {{suite}}

# Lint tracked GDScript (gdlint). Mirrors CI.
lint:
	{{gd_files}} | xargs -r gdlint

# Format tracked GDScript in place (gdformat).
format:
	{{gd_files}} | xargs -r gdformat

# Import assets (needed on a fresh clone or wiped .godot/ before headless runs).
import:
	bin/godot --headless --path . --import

# Export and package a client beta zip for the given OS (macos|linux). Override SERVER_IP
# (env) to embed the current server address in the tester README.
build-client OS SERVER_IP_ARG="":
	bin/build-client {{OS}} "{{SERVER_IP_ARG}}"

# Install Godot export templates for the pinned project version. Run once per
# machine before exporting local client builds.
install-export-templates:
	bin/install-export-templates

# Install addon dependencies declared in plug.gd via gd-plug. Idempotent; run
# automatically by `just test` and `just edit`. Delegates to bin/install-addons.sh
# (the same script CI runs) so install logic lives in one place.
install:
	bin/install-addons.sh

# Pre-PR preflight: everything CI checks (lint + format-check + import + test).
check:
	{{gd_files}} | xargs -r gdformat --check
	{{gd_files}} | xargs -r gdlint
	just import
	just test

# Install the pre-commit hooks (one-time onboarding step).
hooks:
	pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install

# Run the bin/godot version-gate shell test + CI Godot installer test (no Godot needed).
test-godot-gate:
	bash tests/test_godot_version_check.sh
	bash tests/test_install_godot_ci.sh

# Preview a map standalone with a free-fly camera (no menu/lobby/network).
# `just preview` -> default map; `just preview arena3` -> that map;
# `just preview arena3 warm` -> that map + preset. In-preview: [ ] cycle map, P cycle preset.
preview map="" preset="":
	#!/usr/bin/env bash
	set -euo pipefail
	args=()
	[ -n "{{ map }}" ] && args+=("--map={{ map }}")
	[ -n "{{ preset }}" ] && args+=("--preset={{ preset }}")
	# Expand the array only when non-empty. macOS ships bash 3.2, where a plain
	# "${args[@]}" on an empty array under `set -u` errors with "unbound variable";
	# the guard sidesteps that while keeping each flag a single quoted argv entry.
	if [ ${#args[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
		bin/godot --path . res://scenes/dev/map_preview.tscn -- "${args[@]}"
	else
		bin/godot --path . res://scenes/dev/map_preview.tscn --
	fi

tool_use_result.stdout:

content.text: I found something important: the README **already has** a `## Testing` section (lines 88–102) documenting exactly how to run the suite (`just test`, `just test test_weapon`, and the raw headless command). So the transcript's premise — that the README doesn't explain it — is inaccurate. I'll file an accurate issue reflecting the real state rather than a false "it's missing" claim.

input.title: [spike-test] README: review/improve the "Running Tests" documentation
input.body:
## Context

The original request was: *"The README does not explain how to run the test suite. We should add a short Running Tests section so new contributors know the command to use."*

## Finding during investigation

The README **already documents** how to run the test suite. There is a `## Testing` section (README.md lines 88–102) that covers:

- `just test` — full GdUnit4 suite
- `just test test_weapon` — a single suite
- The raw headless equivalent:
  ```bash
  /Applications/Godot.app/Contents/MacOS/Godot --headless --path . \
    -s addons/gdUnit4/bin/GdUnitCmdTool.gd --ignoreHeadlessMode -a tests
  ```

Test commands are also referenced in the `## Developer setup` section (`just test`, `just check`).

## Suggested follow-up

Since the documentation already exists, the original "it's missing" premise no longer applies. Possible refinements instead:

- Consider renaming the section heading from `## Testing` to `## Running Tests` if that wording is preferred for discoverability.
- Optionally cross-link the `## Developer setup` and `## Testing` sections so new contributors land on the commands quickly.

If the existing `## Testing` section is considered sufficient, this issue can simply be closed as already-addressed.

Filed automatically by make-an-issue spike 002 — safe to close.

content.text:
Filed successfully.

- **Issue number:** #89
- **URL:** https://github.com/pulsedemon/netshooter/issues/89

One note worth flagging: the transcript's premise was inaccurate. The README **already has** a `## Testing` section (lines 88–102) documenting `just test`, `just test test_weapon`, and the raw headless command. Rather than file a false "it's missing" issue, I wrote it up accurately — documenting what already exists and suggesting optional refinements (e.g., renaming the heading to "Running Tests" for discoverability), with a note that it can be closed as already-addressed.
